title: cornus kousa subsp. kousa
intro: Cornus kousa subsp. kousa, also known as the Asian dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its showy white or pink flowers and red berries. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cornus kousa subsp. kousa include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
